name: Corneal Dystrophy
description: Corneal dystrophy is a hereditary condition causing non-inflammatory lipid, mineral, or epithelial deposits in the cornea. It appears as white or grey opacities in the cornea, usually bilaterally. Most forms cause limited vision loss but some progress to ulceration.
symptoms: Occasional corneal ulceration in erosive form
treatment_overview: Most cases require no treatment beyond monitoring. Corneal erosive dystrophy benefits from topical lubricants and debridement procedures. Surgical keratectomy may be considered for vision-impairing deposits.
prevention_tips: Do not breed dogs with epithelial or stromal dystrophy causing visual impairment
